plugins.name: browzer
plugins.description: Hybrid vector + Graph RAG search across indexed codebases and document workspaces, powered by the browzer CLI. Twenty-four skills covering install/auth, workspace + doc ingestion, code + markdown search, dependency graph, async ingestion polling, workspace management, skill discovery (find-skills), one-shot repo bootstrapping (browzer-bootstraper), and an end-to-end idea-to-merge dev workflow (brainstorming → generate-prd → generate-task → execute-task | execute-with-teams → code-review → receiving-code-review → write-tests → update-docs → feature-acceptance → commit → sync-workspace). Code-review spawns 4 mandatory parallel agents — senior-engineer, software-architect, qa, regression-tester — all receiving diff + browzer deps (forward + reverse) + browzer mentions; the regression-tester runs the canonical pre-push gate at PACKAGE granularity, enumerates failures per-test, and reproduces each against `main` via git stash for accurate `preExistingOnMain` flagging. Receiving-code-review then closes EVERY finding with a 7-step ladder (sonnet → research → opus → research → log to tech-debt) for zero-tech-debt by default. Write-tests authors green coverage AND runs Stryker/mutmut/go-mutesting in the same pass against the final post-fix state. Orchestrated by orchestrate-task-delivery with autonomous/review-gate toggle, mandatory `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS` probe + execution-strategy prompt, and a unified workflow.json state file per feature. Ships a `Stop` hook (orchestrator-autochain.py) that enforces the autonomous chain machine-checkably; universal subagent preamble requires `browzer deps --reverse` blast-radius probing before any edit; feature-acceptance §2.5.1 anti-soft-override regex blocks autonomous flips of dashboard/browser/`/ask`/`/sync` metrics without operator reply.
